label: Narrow-shell 3BR / baths DISTRIBUTED with SIDE CORRIDOR and service strip — the canonical narrow parti: bedroom column west, 1.0 m corridor center, wet/service strip east; great_room (entry) spans the full front, master spans the full rear. Open great_room + kitchen (gr style). Tuned for the 6.5 x 13.0 m canonical shell.

adjacencies.note: kitchen occupies the front of the east strip, open to the great_room over a counter (gr style)
the corridor's front end mouths into the great_room (codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open)
br2 (west column, first bedroom) doors east into the corridor
br3 (west column, behind br2) doors east into the corridor
standards stack front-to-rear in the west column — wall only
master spans the full rear band — its door sits at the corridor's rear terminus
common T&B (east strip, behind kitchen) doors west into the corridor
kitchen and common stack on the east strip sharing the plumbing wall — stack 1
ensuite is an alcove at master's rear-west corner (west + rear yard venting) — stack 2, interior door from master

soft_proximities.note: pull master's alcove toward the east strip where depth allows, shortening the branch between the two stacks
keep the shared bath directly across the corridor from the rear standard

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_nw_side_corridor_baths_ds_hall_gr
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: The corridor + strip section costs 3.2 + 0.95 + 1.8 = ~6.0 m internal width — exactly the canonical 6.5 m shell's interior. Below that, the strip pinches under 1.8 m and the parti loses to split_columns; fall back rather than relax further. Master capped at 14 m² so the full-width rear band doesn't starve the standards.

notes: 3BR narrow catalog topology #2 — SIDE CORRIDOR with service strip, the
canonical narrow parti. Natural floor band ~80-92 m²; designed against the
canonical test: 10.5 x 17.0 m lot (2.0 m setbacks) → 6.5 x 13.0 m shell
(84.5 m²). Regime: single-detached, no firewalls.
Layout intent (west = left, front = bottom):
Rear:  [ensuite : master (full width)        ]
..     [br3     | HALL | common T&B          ]
..     [br2     | HALL | kitchen             ]
Front: [great_room (entry, full width)       ]
Program: three sub-columns behind the full-width great_room — bedroom column
west (~3.2 m: br2, br3), corridor center (~1.0 m), service strip east
(~1.9 m: kitchen open to great over the counter, then common T&B). The
corridor dies into master's door; master rewards the walk with the full
6.5 m rear band and rear-yard windows. Dirty kitchen + service sit in the
2.0 m rear setback behind the strip.
Plumbing: kitchen+common share the east strip wall (stack 1); ensuite is
master's rear-west alcove (stack 2) — distributed, with a soft pull to
shorten the branch. Ventilation: west column vents the west yard, strip
vents east, master vents rear — no landlocked rooms (no-firewall premise).
Scaling: at the generous shell (7.0 x 14+) the strip widens to a real galley
and the corridor gains storage; at compact it loses to split_columns —
fallback declared.
Carport policy (deferred): widening one side setback 2.0 → 3.0 m (lot +1.0 m)
adds the carport later; mirror so the strip faces the carport side and the
kitchen/dirty-kitchen service path stays on the driveway.
